quality.rationale: 基于测地线守恒量构建三类边界条件的非线性微分方程，经参数化积分得固有时与坐标时显式关系，逻辑链条完整且揭示视界处坐标时发散的真实物理图像

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s15_15.4.md]Problem 15.4. Derive the equations of motion (equations relating $t$ , $r$ and $\tau$ ) for a particle falling radially in the Schwarzschild geometry. Consider the three cases (i) particle released from rest at $r = R$ (ii) particle released from rest at infinity (iii) particle projected inward from infinity with velocity $v_{\inf ty}$ .
origin.answer: Case (i) ($\tilde{E}<1$):Parametric ($\eta=0$ at $r=R$):$r = \frac{R}{2}(1+\cos\eta)$$\tau = \left(\frac{R^3}{8M}\right)^{1/2}(\eta+\sin\eta)$$t = 2M\left[\log\left|\frac{\sqrt{R/2M-1}+\tan(\eta/2)}{\sqrt{R/2M-1}-\tan(\eta/2)}\right| + \sqrt{R/2M-1}\left(\eta + \frac{R}{4M}(\eta+\sin\eta)\right)\right]$Case (ii) ($\tilde{E}=1$):$\tau = -\frac{2}{3}\sqrt{\frac{r^3}{2M}}$$t = -\frac{2}{3}\sqrt{\frac{r^3}{2M}} - 4M\sqrt{\frac{r}{2M}} + 2M\log\left|\frac{\sqrt{r/2M}+1}{\sqrt{r/2M}-1}\right|$Case (iii) ($\tilde{E}>1$):Parametric ($\eta=0$ at $r=0$):$r = \frac{R}{2}(\cos h\eta-1)$$\tau = -\left(\frac{R^3}{8M}\right)^{1/2}(\sin h\eta-\eta)$$t = 2M\left[\log\left|\frac{\sqrt{R/2M+1}+\cot h(\eta/2)}{\sqrt{R/2M+1}-\cot h(\eta/2)}\right| - \sqrt{R/2M+1}\left(\eta + \frac{R}{4M}(\sin h\eta-\eta)\right)\right]
origin.solution: Solution 15.4. From the first integrals of the geodesic equation (see Problem 15.3)$$u_{0} = - \tilde {\mathbf {E}} = \text {c o n s t a n t}$$$$\mathrm {g}^{0 0} \mathrm {u}_{0}^{2} + \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {r r}} \left(\mathrm {u}^{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{2} = - 1$$we get$$u^{0} = \frac {\mathrm {d} t}{\mathrm {d} r} = \frac {\tilde {E}}{1 - 2 M / r} \tag {1}$$$$u^{r} = \frac {d r}{d r} = - \left(\tilde {E}^{2} - 1 + 2 M / r\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}. \tag {2}.$$The minus sign for the square root in Equation (2) corresponds to an infalling particle.Case (i). For this case $\mathrm{dr} / \mathrm{d}\tau = 0$ at $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{R}$ implies $2\mathbf{M} / \mathbf{R} = 1 - \tilde{\mathbf{E}}^2$ , i.e. $\tilde{\mathbf{E}} < 1$ . Thus Equation (2) can be rewritten$$\mathrm {d} \tau = \frac {\mathrm {d r}}{\left(\frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {r}} - \frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {R}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}} \tag {3}$$This can be integrated in closed form to give$$\tau = \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}^{3}}{8 \mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left[ 2 \left(\frac {\mathrm {r}}{\mathrm {R}} - \frac {\mathrm {r}^{2}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} + \cos^{- 1} \left(\frac {2 \mathrm {r}}{\mathrm {R}} - 1\right)\right] \tag {4}$$where the constant of integration has been chosen so that $\tau = 0$ at $r = R$ . It is convenient to rewrite Equation (4) in parametric form by introducing the "cycloid parameter"$$\eta = \cos^{- 1} \left(\frac {2 r}{R} - 1\right)$$so that we have$$r = \frac {1}{2} R (1 + \cos \eta) \quad (\eta = 0 \text {a t} r = R) \tag {5}$$$$\tau = \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}^{3}}{8 \mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} (\eta + \sin \eta). \tag {6}.$$Equation (1) gives$$\begin{array}{l} t = \int \frac {\tilde {E} d \tau}{\left(1 - \frac {2 M}{r}\right)} = \tilde {E} \int \frac {d \tau}{d \eta} \frac {1}{1 - \frac {2 M}{r}} d \eta \\= \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {R}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \int \frac {\left(\frac {\mathrm {R}^{3}}{8 \mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} (1 + \cos \eta) \mathrm {d} \eta}{1 - 4 \mathrm {M} [ \mathrm {R} (1 + \cos \eta) ]^{- 1}}. \\\end{array}$$From a table of integrals, we find$$\frac {t}{2 \mathrm {M}} = \log \left| \frac {\left(\frac {\mathrm {R}}{2 \mathrm {M}} - 1\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} + \tan \left(\frac {\eta}{2}\right)}{\left(\frac {\mathrm {R}}{2 \mathrm {M}} - 1\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} - \tan \left(\frac {\eta}{2}\right)}\right| + \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}}{2 \mathrm {M}} - 1\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left[ \eta + \frac {\mathrm {R}}{4 \mathrm {M}} (\eta + \sin \eta)\right] \tag {7}$$where the constant of integration has been chosen so that $t = 0$ at $\eta = 0$ i.e. at $r = R$ . Note that $t \to \inf ty$ when $\tan(\eta/2) \to (R/2M - 1)^{\frac{1}{2}}$ i.e. when $r \to 2M$ .Case (ii). Here $\tilde{\mathbf{E}} = 1$ and Equation (2) gives$$\tau = - \frac {2}{3} \left(\frac {\mathrm {r}^{3}}{2 \mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} + \text {c o n s t a n t} \tag {8}$$and since$$\frac {\mathrm {d} t}{\mathrm {d} r} = \frac {\mathrm {d} t / \mathrm {d} \tau}{\mathrm {d} r / \mathrm {d} \tau}$$we get$$t = - \frac {2}{3} \left(\frac {r^{3}}{2 M}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} - 4 M \left(\frac {r}{2 M}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} + 2 M \log \left| \frac {\left(\frac {r}{2 M}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} + 1}{\left(\frac {r}{2 M}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} - 1}\right| + \text {c o n s t a n t}. \tag {9}$$Case (iii). By analogy with case (i), choose R such that$$\frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {R}} = \tilde {\mathrm {E}}^{2} - 1 = \left(1 - \mathrm {v}_{\inf ty}^{2}\right)^{- 1} - 1 = \frac {\mathrm {v}_{\inf ty}^{2}}{1 - \mathrm {v}_{\inf ty}^{2}} .$$Then changing the sign of $\mathbf{R}$ in Equation (4) gives$$\tau = - \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}^{3}}{8 \mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left[ 2 \left(\frac {\mathrm {r}}{\mathrm {R}} + \frac {\mathrm {r}^{2}}{\mathrm {R}^{2}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} - \cos h^{- 1} \left(\frac {2 \mathrm {r}}{\mathrm {R}} + 1\right)\right]. \tag {10}$$Note that $\tau = 0$ at $\mathbf{r} = 0$ , and $\tau = -\inf ty$ at $\mathbf{r} = \inf ty$ . Put$$\eta = \cos h^{- 1} \left(\frac {2 r}{R} + 1\right)$$and get$$r = \frac {R}{2} (\cos h \eta - 1) \quad (r = 0 \text {a t} \eta = 0) \tag {11}$$$$\tau = - \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}^{3}}{8 \mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} (\sin h \eta - \eta). \tag {12}.$$The equation corresponding to Equation (7) is$$\frac {t}{2 \mathbb {M}} = \log \left| \frac {\left(\frac {\mathrm {R}}{2 \mathbb {M}} + 1\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} + \cot h \frac {\eta}{2}}{\left(\frac {\mathrm {R}}{2 \mathbb {M}} + 1\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} - \cot h \frac {\eta}{2}}\right| - \left(\frac {\mathrm {R}}{2 \mathbb {M}} + 1\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left[ \eta + \frac {\mathrm {R}}{4 \mathbb {M}} (\sin h \eta - \eta)\right]. \tag {13}$$Note that $t$ goes from $-\inf ty$ to $+\inf ty$ as $r$ goes from $\inf ty$ to $2M$ .

killing_vectors.name: time_translation
killing_vectors.description: 时间平移 Killing 矢量 (∂_t)，对应能量守恒

killing_vectors.name: rot_phi
killing_vectors.description: 绕 φ 轴旋转 Killing 矢量 (∂_φ)，对应角动量守恒
